title: How to make any article as Featured Posts?

content.text: This document is designed to help publishers to feature their posts, an excellent way to highlight important content on your website. Featured posts ensures they gain more visibility and remain prominently displayed at the top of your site.

sections.heading: Steps to make the post as featured:

content.text: Step 1: Log In and Navigate to the Published Section

items.text: Log in to the PubLive Dashboard .
Navigate to Post -> Click Published section to view all your published articles.

content.text: Step 2:Select and Feature an Article

items.text: Choose the articles you want to feature.
Click on the Set as Featured Post option.

content.text: Step 3: Confirm Featured Status

items.text: Once marked as featured, the selected article will:

children.text: Appear with a blue asterisk in the Published section.
Automatically move to the Featured section of the dashboard.

content.text: Step 4: Manage Featured Post Visibility

items.text: Use the drag-and-drop functionality to rearrange the order of featured articles.
The priority numbering is automatically assigned when the user rearranges the items.
Click Save after making changes to ensure the updated order reflects on the website.

content.text: Note:

items.text: A maximum of 20 featured posts can be displayed in the Featured Posts section on the website. Ensure to prioritize your articles accordingly.
Each featured post has a Delete and Preview option visible in the Featured section.
Deleting an article from the Featured section will not delete the article itself. It will only remove the post from the featured list, keeping it available in the Published section.

content.text: Best Practices:

items.text: Prioritize High-Quality Content : Feature articles that are well-researched, engaging, and valuable to your audience. Highlighting subpar content can diminish the effectiveness of the feature.
Regularly Update Featured Posts : Keep the featured section dynamic by rotating articles frequently. Showcase seasonal or trending content to maintain audience interest.
Align with Business Goals: Select articles that align with your website’s objectives, such as driving sales, increasing awareness, or promoting specific campaigns.
Use Engaging Visuals: Ensure featured articles have eye-catching thumbnails and titles to immediately capture visitor attention.
Track Performance: Use analytics tools to monitor the traffic and engagement metrics of featured articles. Optimize your selection based on these insights.

content.text: Use Cases :

items.text: Product Launch Announcements: Showcase new product launches or updates to drive awareness and sales.
Trending Topics: Feature articles covering trending news, popular topics, or viral stories to boost traffic.
Evergreen Content: Promote timeless, high-value articles that continuously attract readership over time.
Event Promotions: Draw attention to upcoming events, webinars, or workshops by featuring related posts.
Content Series: Spotlight parts of a multi-article series, encouraging readers to explore related articles.

content.text: Benefits:

items.text: Enhanced Visibility: Featuring posts ensures important content remains prominently displayed, capturing the audience’s attention immediately upon visiting the website.
Increased Engagement: By highlighting compelling content, featured posts encourage users to click and interact more with the website.
I mproved SEO Performance: Featuring frequently updated, high-quality articles can improve your website's search engine rankings by signaling relevance and freshness.
Audience Targeting: Allows publishers to direct readers to content that meets specific objectives, such as promotions or announcements.
Better Content Organization: Featured posts help create a structured and visually appealing layout, making it easier for readers to navigate.

content.text: All featured posts will appear at the top of your website in the order specified in the Featured section of the dashboard. This allows you to prioritize and control which articles are most visible to your audience.

title: How does Smart Embeds work in Editor?

content.text: Smart Embed feature refers to the ability to display content from one website or application within another, allowing for a more visually appealing and interactive experience for users. It can be easily done by copy-paste the social posts link into the Editor of the PubLive Dashboard. When you paste the link of a social media post, the Smart embed automatically generates a preview of the webpage, showing its title, description, and image. This gives viewers a glimpse of the content before they decide to click on the link.

Smart embeds can also display other types of media, such as videos, audio players, interactive maps, slideshows, and more. They enhance the user experience by providing a more dynamic and engaging way to interact with content without leaving the website.
To get started, copy the social post link/URL and paste it into the editor

sections.heading: How does this feature help Digital Publishers?

content.text: Increased Engagement: Smart embeds make content more visually appealing and interactive. When users encounter visually enticing previews, such as images, videos, or interactive elements, they are more likely to engage with the content and click on the link to view the full content. This increased engagement can lead to higher website traffic and more views for the publisher's content.

Improved User Experience : By providing additional context and information through rich previews, publishers can improve the overall user experience. Readers can quickly grasp what the content is about and decide whether they want to explore it further. This saves users' time and makes them more likely to consume the content.

Better Content Promotion: When publishers share their content on various platforms with smart embeds, it becomes easier for others to share that content with their audiences. The appealing previews make it more likely for people to share the content, leading to organic promotion and wider exposure.

Cross-Platform Compatibility: Smart embeds work across different devices and platforms, ensuring that the content is accessible and displayed optimally on various devices, including desktops, smartphones, and tablets.

SEO Benefits: Some smart embeds utilize metadata markup, like Twitter Cards, which can enhance search engine optimization (SEO). These metadata tags provide search engines with valuable information about the content, potentially improving its search rankings and visibility.

Media Support: Smart embeds can display various types of media, such as images, videos, audio players, maps, and slideshows. This versatility allows publishers to present their content in engaging ways that cater to different preferences.
Overall, Smart embeds contribute to a more user-friendly and visually appealing online experience, making it easier for people to discover and engage with content from various sources.

title: Permalink Editing

content.text: We are thrilled to announce a new feature that will enable the publishers to update the link of the published article: Permalink Editing Feature. Permalinks are the permanent URLs that point to a specific article, and allowing edits to them after publication can help in various scenarios, such as fixing typos, improving SEO, or updating outdated information without breaking existing links. Note: The Permalink Editing feature in the dashboard is accessible to Owners and Admin only To get started, go to the published section. You can find the Edit Permalink option in the extended menu as shown below. Click the Edit Permalink for the article to change the URL. It will open a modal where you can provide the new URL as shown below Current Permalink - Current URL of the article Enter Permalink - Where you need to provide the new URL/link of the article. If there is any issue in the format, give a warning in red. Once done and if the provided link has no issue in its format then the Update Permalink button will be enabled. Now, click the button to update Once the Update Permalink button is clicked, a notification will be displayed as shown below. Now the article will have the updated link. Note: The old link of an article will be redirected to the new link. If anyone visits the old link, will be redirected automatically to the new URL without breaking or causing errors. These Permalinks will also be visible below the Permalink Field for the article in the dashboard under edit mode as shown below How does this feature help Digital Publishers? Correction of Errors: The ability to edit permalinks allows authors and editors to correct typos, grammatical errors, or other mistakes in URLs without having to delete and recreate the entire article or content item. Improved SEO: Permalink editing can be valuable for search engine optimization (SEO). If you discover that a permalink could be better optimized for search engines, you can make adjustments without losing the SEO benefits already gained from the existing content. Update Outdated Information: Over time, content may become outdated. With permalink editing, you can update the content and adjust the permalink to reflect the changes while maintaining the integrity of existing links. Content Reorganization: If you want to reorganize your content structure, permalink editing allows you to change the URL structure to better fit your requirements without breaking existing links. Enhanced User Experience: Permalink editing helps maintain a positive user experience by ensuring that users always land on the correct and up-to-date content, even if they access it through older links or bookmarks. Preventing Broken Links: Instead of creating a new article with a corrected URL and potentially leaving behind broken links to the old one, you can edit the permalink and set up redirects to ensure a seamless transition for users and search engines. Flexibility in Content Creation: It provides content creators with more flexibility in naming articles or pages, allowing for creativity and optimization without being constrained by initially chosen URLs. Content Maintenance: As your content evolves, you can use permalink editing to keep your URLs in sync with the content, reducing confusion for both users and content creators. Implementation of a permalink editing feature for published articles can enhance the flexibility and maintenance of your website's content while preserving a positive user experience. However, it should be used judiciously and with careful consideration of its potential impacts on SEO and user navigation.

How it benefits the digital publishers

1. Authenticity and Trust UGC is prized for its authenticity. In a digital era saturated with branded content, user-generated material provides a refreshing authenticity that resonates with audiences. The trust users place in content generated by their peers contributes to a more credible and genuine online environment.

2. Diverse Perspectives: Embracing UGC enables digital publishers to tap into a wealth of diverse perspectives, experiences, and voices. This diversity enriches the content landscape, making it more inclusive and reflective of the varied interests and backgrounds of the audience.

3. Enhanced Engagement: UGC fosters higher levels of engagement as it invites active participation from the audience. Digital platforms that encourage users to contribute content, share experiences, and participate in discussions benefit from increased user interaction, longer time spent on the platform, and higher click-through rates.

4. Cost-Effective Content Creation: Digital publishers often face the challenge of producing a constant stream of fresh and engaging content. UGC provides a cost-effective solution as users contribute content voluntarily, reducing the burden on the publisher's resources while maintaining a steady flow of material.

5. Community Building: UGC plays a pivotal role in building a sense of community around a digital platform. When users feel their contributions are valued, it fosters a sense of belonging and encourages sustained participation, creating a vibrant and loyal community.

7. SEO Benefits: Search engines favor dynamic and regularly updated content. UGC, when appropriately managed and curated, contributes to a continuously evolving digital landscape, improving search engine rankings and driving organic traffic to the publisher's website.

Overall, Incorporating user-generated content into digital publishing strategies enhances authenticity, diversifies content offerings, and fosters a more engaged and loyal audience. Digital publishers can harness the power of UGC to build vibrant online communities, improve search visibility, and create a more dynamic and responsive digital presence.

title: Why should you not create tags on the go?

content.text: Before we tell you why you shouldn't create tags on the go, let us acknowledge the importance of tags. Tags are crucial for organizing and categorizing content. Your readers can easily explore all posts in their interested tags.

Tags are good for you but the problem begins when you don't manage your tags. In fact, if you create too many posts in short intervals, you might be tempted to create a tag everytime you don't find the tag you are looking for!
The problems with too many tags are the following:

items.text: Thin Content: However, since the newly created tags might not have a lot of content associated, they become "thin content". Further, Google may penalize sites with a high number of thin content pages, impacting your site's authority and ranking.
Duplicate Content: Redundant tags can lead to duplicate content issues since you might have similar tags associated with different pieces of content of the same topic.

content.text: Neither does Google like thin content nor does it like duplicate content.
As such, we recommend the following best practices

items.text: Predefined Tag List

content.text: Identify the most important keywords for your brand and create tags for the different types of content you'd create for those keywords. Try to stick to these tags to avoid tag redundancy.

items.text: Regular Tag Audit

content.text: Conduct regular tag audits to find duplicate tags and merge similar ones. You can also delete unused tags to avoid thin content. This will help you keep a clean and efficient content management system.

items.text: Training

content.text: Train your content creators on the importance of tag management. Share the best practices with them to ensure your website follows best practices.

title: Introducing Log Activity Feature to manage all your logs and activities

content.text: Overview

The Log Activity feature, located in the Configuration section under "Log Activity," is essential for maintaining a comprehensive record of various activities within your platform. This feature is exclusively accessible to Owners and Admins, ensuring only authorized personnel can manage and review these logs.
To access the Log Activity feature:

items.text: Navigate to the Configuration section.
Select Log Activity from the menu.

content.text: Adding a New Activity Log
Owners and Admins can add new activity logs by following these steps:

items.text: Click the Add Activity button.
Fill in the Description field with a detailed account of the activity.
Add relevant Tags to categorize and easily search for the log later.
Specify the Time of the activity. This can include past times, allowing for backdated logging of activities.

content.text: Added logs will be recorded in the Log Activity as shown below.
Search & Filter for Tags
Users can search by Tags in the search field to find relevants log activities.
Additionally, can filter the logs by custom date range
The Log Activity feature serves multiple purposes:

items.text: Record-Keeping: Maintain a detailed history of significant activities and changes within the platform.
Accountability: Ensure all actions taken by the members are documented by Owners and Admins for future reference.
Transparency: Provide a clear and accessible trail of activities that can be reviewed as needed.
Enhanced Oversight:

children.text: Publishers can maintain a clear and organized record of all significant activities, ensuring nothing is overlooked.
Helps in auditing and compliance by providing a transparent log of actions.

items.text: Improved Accountability:

children.text: By logging activities, Owners and Admins can be held accountable.
Promotes responsible management practices within the organization.

items.text: Efficient Troubleshooting:

children.text: Logs can be referred to when troubleshooting issues, helping to quickly identify and address the root causes of problems.
Provides a historical context that can aid in understanding the sequence of events leading up to an issue.

items.text: Data-Driven Decisions:

children.text: Detailed logs provide valuable insights into platform usage and management activities, supporting data-driven decision-making.
Enables the identification of patterns and trends that can inform strategic planning.

content.text: The Log Activity feature is a powerful tool for Owners and Admins, enabling meticulous record-keeping, enhanced accountability, and improved oversight. By providing a detailed account of activities, this feature supports transparent and responsible management practices, ultimately benefiting the entire publishing operation.

title: Understanding Bounce Rate Errors in Your Email Newsletters

content.text: Bounce rate refers to the percentage of emails that could not be delivered to the recipient’s inbox. These emails are "bounced back" by the receiving mail server and are usually caused by invalid or unreachable email addresses.
A healthy bounce rate is below 2% . Higher rates can:

items.text: Harm your sender reputation
Reduce email deliverability
Get your domain or IP flagged as spam

content.text: We recommend regular list cleanup to keep your bounce rate low.
Here are the most common bounce subtypes and diagnostic messages , with plain-language explanations and what you should do.

rows: Bounce Subtype
Diagnostic Code / Message (Example)
What It Means
What You Should Do
OnAccountSuppressionList
Amazon SES did not send the message to this address because it is on the suppression list...
This email address previously hard bounced or marked messages as spam. It’s now automatically suppressed by Amazon SES.
Review and remove these addresses from your mailing list. See SES Suppression List Guide if needed.
MailboxNotFound
smtp; 550-5.1.1 The email account you tried to reach does not exist.
The email address doesn’t exist — likely due to a typo, fake address, or deleted account.
Remove the address from your list. If input came from a form, enable double opt-in to prevent typos.
MailboxInactive
smtp; 550-5.2.1 The email account you tried to reach is inactive.
The email account exists but is temporarily disabled or abandoned.
Wait and retry once if expected to reactivate. Otherwise, remove it.
MailboxUnavailable
smtp; 550 Requested action not taken: mailbox unavailable
The mailbox is currently unavailable , possibly due to technical or server issues.
Retry a few times. If it continues to bounce, treat it as a hard bounce and remove.
MailboxFull
smtp; 552 5.2.2 The email account is over quota.
The recipient's mailbox is full and can’t accept new messages.
Retry later. If the bounce persists over multiple days, consider removing or flagging.
InvalidRecipient / UserNotFound
smtp; 550 5.1.1 <email@example.com>: user does not exist
The domain exists, but the specific user doesn't. This often occurs when employees leave companies.
Remove from list. Optionally flag for manual review if it's a known contact.
Typo / Format Issue
smtp; ... double-checking the recipient's email address for typos or unnecessary spaces
The address may be malformed — extra characters, wrong domain, etc.
Check for typos or cleanup issues. Remove if not fixable.
DomainDoesNotExist
smtp; 552 1 Requested mail action aborted, mailbox not found
The entire domain (after @) does not exist.
Remove from list immediately. May be a fake or mistyped domain.
Unknown Google User
smtp; 550-5.1.1 ... <https://support.google.com/mail/?p=NoSuchUser >
Google is reporting that the user does not exist.
Remove from list. Cross-check if it's a valid user from your records.
Disabled Google User
smtp; 550-5.2.1 ... <https://support.google.com/mail/?p=DisabledUser >
The Gmail account is inactive or disabled by the user or Google.
Remove from list unless you expect the user to reactivate their account.

sections.heading: What Should I Do With Bounced Emails?

content.text: Here’s how to handle bounced addresses on your end:

items.text: ❌ Remove any email flagged as "does not exist", "inactive", or "suppressed".
✅ Enable double opt-in so users confirm their email is correct.
Use an email validation tool to pre-check for invalid or inactive domains.
Don’t buy email lists.
Don’t send to old/inactive lists without reconfirmation.
Avoid sending repeated emails to previously bounced addresses.

content.text: A high bounce rate can cause:

items.text: Poor inbox placement
Delivery throttling or blacklisting
Messages going straight to spam

content.text: Keeping your list clean improves engagement and protects your brand reputation.
If you're unsure how to interpret a bounce error, or want help cleaning your list, feel free to contact our support team with:

items.text: The full error message
Bounce subtype (if available)
Email address in question

title: Domain Verification on Publive

content.text: To send newsletters through Publive, you’ll need to verify your domain by configuring DNS records. This ensures that emails are delivered securely and are less likely to land in spam.
When you send newsletters from Publive, we want your readers to trust that emails actually come from your domain. Domain verification helps with:

items.text: SPF (Sender Policy Framework) Prevents spoofing by telling recipient servers which mail servers are authorized to send on behalf of your domain.
DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) Adds a cryptographic signature to every email, so recipients can confirm the email hasn’t been altered and is really from your domain.

DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance) Builds on SPF and DKIM. Lets you define how recipient servers handle emails that fail authentication (monitor, quarantine, or reject).

content.text: ✅ Together, these improve deliverability, protect your brand reputation, and keep newsletters out of spam.

items.text: Go to Integrations

children.text: From the Publive dashboard, click Configuration > Integrations .
Locate the Newsletter Setup card and click Set Up .

items.text: Enter Your Sender Email

children.text: Add the email address you want to send newsletters from (e.g., news@yourdomain.com ).
Publive will detect the domain ( yourdomain.com ) and begin the verification process.

items.text: View DNS Records

children.text: Publive will generate the required SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records for your domain.
You’ll see these in a table with copy buttons for easy setup.

sections.heading: Adding Records in Your DNS Provider

content.text: Example is of Cloudflare , but steps are similar for GoDaddy, Namecheap, or others.

items.text: Log in to your DNS provider.
Select your domain.
Go to the DNS Records section.
Click Add Record .

children.text: For SPF/DKIM : Choose TXT .
For DMARC : Choose TXT with host _dmarc .
Enter the Host/Name and Value/Content exactly as shown in Publive.

items.text: Save changes.

content.text: ⏳ DNS propagation may take up to 24 hours.

items.text: After adding records, return to Publive’s setup page.
Click Verify DNS Records .
Status will update from Pending → Verified once propagation completes.

children.text: If verified, you’ll see green checkmarks next to each record.
If still pending, wait a few hours and try again.

items.text: Not verified after 24 hours?

children.text: Double-check you copied Host and Value correctly.
Ensure there are no duplicate SPF records (only one allowed per domain).
Make sure DMARC host is _dmarc , not your full domain.

items.text: Still stuck? Reach out to your DNS provider support or contact Publive support with a screenshot of your DNS records.

content.text: Once your domain is verified:

items.text: You’re ready to send newsletters from your domain.
Test by sending a preview email to your personal inbox.
Monitor your deliverability and bounce rates from the Analytics tab .

content.text: That’s it! Your domain is now authenticated, ensuring higher trust and better inbox placement for your newsletters.

1. Can I use a Gmail/Yahoo/Outlook address instead of my domain email? No. Free mailbox providers (like Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook) do not allow adding custom SPF/DKIM/DMARC records. You must use a custom domain email (e.g., news@yourdomain.com ).
2. Can I verify more than one domain? Yes. Publive supports multiple verified domains. Each newsletter campaign must be sent from one verified domain.
3. How long does verification take? Usually within minutes, but DNS changes can take up to 24 hours to propagate worldwide.
4. What if I already have SPF/DKIM/DMARC records? You can merge Publive’s required values with your existing records:

items.text: SPF → Only one SPF record is allowed. Combine values into a single TXT record.
DKIM → Multiple DKIM records are fine; just add Publive’s.
DMARC → You can only have one DMARC policy; adjust it if needed.

content.text: 5. Do I need to update records again later? No. Once verified, you don’t need to update unless you change DNS providers or domains.
6. What happens if I don’t set up domain verification? You won’t be able to send newsletters from Publive. Even if attempted, unauthenticated emails are more likely to be rejected or marked as spam.

title: How to Get Your Razorpay API Keys

content.text: To integrate Razorpay with any platform, you’ll need to generate your API Key ID and API Key Secret from the Razorpay dashboard. Here’s how you can do it step by step:

sections.heading: Step-by-Step: Generate API Keys on Razorpay

items.text: Visit https://dashboard.razorpay.com
Enter your registered email and password to sign in.

sections.heading: 2. Go to the API Keys Section

Under Settings, select “API Keys”

content.text: You can also view Razorpay’s official documentation here:? Razorpay API Keys Guide

items.text: Click on “Generate Key”
Razorpay will display two values:

children.text: Key ID – Safe to share
Key Secret – Sensitive (copy immediately, shown only once)

content.text: ⚠️ Important: Make sure to copy and securely store the Key Secret. If you lose it, you’ll need to generate a new key pair.

items.text: Go back to the API Keys section
Click “Regenerate Key”
A new Key ID + Key Secret will be created (your old keys will stop working)

title: Addressing "Not Indexed" Pages in Google Search Console

content.text: This document provides guidance for publishers whose Google Search Console (GSC) reports show a significant number of pages not indexed due to the following reasons:
1. Crawled - currently not indexed

sections.heading: 3. Alternative page with proper canonical tag

content.text: For each scenario, we’ll explore the causes, troubleshooting steps, and actionable solutions to help improve indexing.
What It Means:
Google has crawled the page but has chosen not to index it. This typically happens when Google determines the page doesn’t provide enough value or relevance to be indexed.
Common Causes:

items.text: Low-quality or thin content.
Duplicate content without proper canonical tags.
Pages with little or no traffic or engagement.
Technical issues like slow loading times or poor mobile usability.

content.text: Steps to Resolve:
1. Evaluate Content Quality:

items.text: Ensure the page has unique, high-quality, and valuable content.
Avoid thin content or pages with little substantive information.

content.text: 2. Check for Duplicate Content:

items.text: Use canonical tags to indicate the preferred version of duplicate pages.
Consolidate similar pages if necessary.

content.text: 3. Improve Page Performance:

items.text: Optimize page speed and ensure mobile-friendliness.
Use tools like Google PageSpeed Insights to identify and fix performance issues.

content.text: 4. Enhance Internal Linking:

items.text: Ensure the page is linked from other relevant pages on your site.
Use a clear site structure to help Google understand the page’s importance.

content.text: 5. Monitor and Resubmit:

items.text: Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check the page’s status.
Request re-indexing after making improvements.

content.text: What It Means:
Google has discovered the page (e.g., through sitemaps or internal links) but hasn’t crawled it yet. This often happens due to crawl budget limitations or low priority.
Common Causes:

items.text: Large websites with many low-priority pages.
Poor site architecture or lack of internal linking.
Crawl budget issues (common for very large sites).
Blocked by robots.txt or noindex tags (though this would typically show a different status).

content.text: Steps to Resolve:
1. Optimize Crawl Budget:

items.text: Prioritize important pages in your sitemap.
Remove or noindex low-value pages (e.g., filters, session IDs).

content.text: 2. Improve Internal Linking:

items.text: Ensure important pages are linked from high-traffic pages.
Use a clear and logical site hierarchy.

content.text: 3. Submit a Sitemap:

items.text: Create and submit an XML sitemap to GSC.
Regularly update the sitemap to reflect new or updated pages.

content.text: 4. Check for Blocking Issues:

items.text: Ensure the page isn’t blocked by robots.txt or noindex tags.
Use the URL Inspection Tool to verify crawlability.

content.text: 5. Monitor and Request Crawling:

items.text: Use the URL Inspection Tool to request indexing for important pages.
Be patient, as crawling may take time for large sites.

sections.heading: Alternative Page with Proper Canonical Tag

content.text: What It Means:

Google has identified another page as the canonical (preferred) version of the content, so the current page is not indexed. This is not necessarily an issue if the canonical tag is correctly implemented.
Common Causes:

items.text: The page is a duplicate or near-duplicate of another page.
The canonical tag points to another URL.
The page is intentionally marked as non-canonical.

content.text: Steps to Resolve:
1. Verify Canonical Tags:

items.text: Ensure the canonical tag points to the correct preferred version of the page.
Use the URL Inspection Tool to check canonicalization.

content.text: 2. Consolidate Duplicate Content:

items.text: If the page is a duplicate, consider merging it with the canonical version.
Redirect the non-canonical page to the canonical version if appropriate.

content.text: 3. Check for Conflicting Signals:

items.text: Ensure there are no conflicting signals (e.g., hreflang tags pointing to different pages).
Use consistent internal linking to the canonical page.

content.text: 4. Monitor Indexing Status:

items.text: Use GSC to monitor the indexing status of the canonical page.
Ensure the canonical page is indexed and performing well.

content.text: 5. Reassess Content Strategy:

items.text: If the page is intentionally non-canonical, ensure it serves a purpose (e.g., for internal use or specific audiences).
Consider removing or noindexing pages that don’t need to be indexed.

content.text: General Best Practices for All Scenarios:
Regularly Audit Your Site : Use tools like Screaming Frog or Sitebulb to identify technical issues.

items.text: Monitor GSC Reports: Keep an eye on the Index Coverage report for trends and anomalies.
Focus on Quality : Prioritize high-quality, unique, and valuable content.
Stay Updated : Follow Google’s guidelines and algorithm updates to ensure compliance.

content.text: A 404 error indicates that the page no longer exists or cannot be found on your website. This happens when:

items.text: The page has been deleted.
The URL has been changed without a proper redirect.
There are broken internal or external links pointing to the page.
Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs with 404 errors.
Check if the page was intentionally removed or if it’s a broken link.
Restore the Page (if needed):

children.text: If the page was accidentally deleted, restore it from backups or recreate it.
Ensure the URL matches the original.

items.text: Fix Broken Links:

children.text: Use tools like Screaming Frog or Ahrefs to find and fix broken internal links.
Update or remove external links pointing to the 404 page.

items.text: Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check if the 404 error is resolved.
Regularly monitor the Coverage Report for new 404 errors.

content.text: What It Means:
This error occurs when a page has a redirect (e.g., 301 or 302) instead of serving the content directly. While redirects are not inherently bad, they can cause issues if:

items.text: There are multiple redirects (redirect chains).
The redirect points to an irrelevant or broken page.
The redirect is unnecessary.
Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs with redirects.
Use tools like Screaming Frog or Redirect Path (Chrome extension) to analyze redirect chains.
Remove Unnecessary Redirects:

children.text: If the redirect is unnecessary, remove it and serve the content directly.

items.text: Shorten Redirect Chains:

children.text: Ensure there are no more than one or two redirects in a chain.
Directly redirect the old URL to the final destination.

items.text: Update Internal Links:

children.text: Update internal links to point to the final destination URL instead of the redirected URL.

items.text: Check External Links:

children.text: If possible, ask external websites to update their links to the final destination.

items.text: Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check if the redirect issue is resolved.
Regularly audit your site for redirect chains.

content.text: This error occurs when a page is excluded from indexing because it has a noindex tag. This tag tells search engines not to index the page. Common causes include:

items.text: The page was intentionally marked as noindex like reader login page, no result found page (404).
If the tag page contains less than 4 posts it will be mark as noindex.
The page is a duplicate or low-priority page.
Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs excluded by noindex tags.
Check the page’s HTML source code or HTTP headers for the noindex tag.
Remove the Noindex Tag (if needed):

children.text: If the page should be indexed, remove the noindex tag.
Example: Remove <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> from the page’s <head> section.

items.text: Check for Conflicting Signals:

children.text: Ensure the page is not blocked by robots.txt or other directives.
Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check for crawlability.

items.text: Reindex the Page:

children.text: Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to request indexing after removing the noindex tag.

items.text: Audit Low-Priority Pages:

children.text: If the page is intentionally noindexed , ensure it serves a purpose (e.g., thank-you pages, internal tools).

items.text: Use GSC’s Coverage Report to confirm the page is no longer excluded.
Monitor the page’s indexing status and traffic.

content.text: Regular Audits : Use tools like Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, or SEMrush to audit your site for errors.
Monitor GSC Reports : Regularly check the Coverage Report and Indexing Report in GSC.
Fix Errors Promptly : Address errors as soon as they are detected to avoid negative impacts on SEO.

items.text: Crawled - currently not indexed
Discovered - currently not indexed
Alternative page with proper canonical tag

content.text: By addressing the specific reasons for "Not Indexed" pages and implementing the recommended solutions, publishers can improve their site’s indexing and overall search performance. Regularly monitor GSC and make adjustments as needed to ensure optimal visibility in search results.

title: Common AMP Issues, Causes, and Solutions

content.text: AMP (Accelerated Mobile Pages) is a framework that enforces strict guidelines to ensure high-speed performance and smooth rendering of web pages. However, certain issues commonly occur due to violations of AMP’s rules. This document outlines common AMP errors, their
causes, and solutions, along with best practices to prevent them.
Cause:

items.text: The tag lacks a src attribute or contains an empty value.
The src URL is dynamically inserted via JavaScript, which is not allowed in AMP.

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Ensure every tag has a valid src attribute.
Use instead of : Tag sample:

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Always specify a valid src in <amp-img>.
Validate the page using AMP Validator .

content.text: Cause:

items.text: AMP does not support traditional JavaScript (<script> tags) for security and performance reasons.

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Use AMP components instead, such as: <amp-carousel> for sliders <amp-bind> for interactivity <amp-analytics> for tracking.

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Replace standard JavaScript functionalities with AMP components .

sections.heading: 3. Disallowed Attribute or Attribute Value Present in HTML Tag

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Use of non-AMP attributes (e.g., onclick, onmouseover).

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Remove non-AMP attributes or use AMP alternatives: Tag Sample: <button on="tap:AMP.setState({modalOpen: true})">Click Me</button>

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Refer to the AMP Attribute Guidelines before implementation.

sections.heading: 4. Only amp-boilerplate and amp-custom <style> Tags Are Allowed

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Custom styles are placed outside <style amp-custom>.

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Move all custom CSS inside <style amp-custom>: Code Sample: <style amp-custom> body { background: #fff; } </style>

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Make sure all custom styles are placed within <style amp-custom> and avoid using external CSS files.

sections.heading: 5. A Mandatory Attribute is Missing from an HTML Tag

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Certain AMP elements require mandatory attributes (e.g., <amp-img> needs width, height, and layout).

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Add the missing attributes: Tag Sample: <amp-img src="image.jpg" width="500" height="300" layout="responsive"></amp-img>

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Follow the AMP Component Documentation .

sections.heading: 6. CSS Syntax Error in <span> or <style amp-custom>

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Invalid CSS rules inside <style amp-custom>

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Remove or correct invalid CSS properties.

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Use AMP CSS Validator to verify correctness.

sections.heading: 7. Usage of !important CSS Qualifier is Not Allowed

content.text: Cause:

items.text: AMP prohibits !important due to style manipulation restrictions.

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Remove !important and adjust styles accordingly.

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Adhere to AMP CSS Restrictions

sections.heading: 8. A Tag on This Page Requires an AMP Component <script> Tag, Which is Missing

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Missing <script> for AMP components used (e.g., <amp-carousel> needs amp-carousel script)

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Include the required <script> tag inside <head>: Tag Sample: <script async custom-element="amp-carousel" src="https://cdn.ampproject.org/v0/amp-carousel-0.2.js"></script>

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Check required scripts for each AMP component.

sections.heading: 9. Invalid URL Protocol in src or href Attribute

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Non-secure URLs (http:// instead of https://)

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Ensure all URLs use the secure HTTPS protocol to comply with AMP guidelines.

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Enforce https:// for all resources

sections.heading: 10. The Parent Tag of <picture> Must be <noscript>

content.text: Cause:

items.text: <picture> is used incorrectly in AMP

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Wrap <picture> inside <noscript>: Tag Sample: <noscript> <picture> <source srcset="image.jpg" type="image/webp"> </picture> </noscript>

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Follow AMP Guidelines for Images .

sections.heading: 11. Tag <amp-story> Has a Disallowed Child Tag

content.text: Cause:

items.text: Invalid child elements inside <amp-story>

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Use only allowed elements such as <amp-story-page>, <amp-story-grid-layer>, and <amp-img>

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Review the AMP Story Documentation

sections.heading: 12. Malformed URL Found in href Attribute of <a> Tag

content.text: Cause:

items.text: The href attribute contains an invalid URL, such as missing a protocol or malformed structure.

content.text: Solution:

items.text: Ensure the URL in href is properly formatted, including https:// Tag Sample: Valid Link

content.text: Prevention:

items.text: Validate URLs before adding them to <a> tags
Use AMP Validator to check for errors.

content.text: By following these best practices and using AMP-compliant elements, you can prevent
common AMP errors, improve website performance, and maintain a smooth user experience.
Always validate your AMP pages using:

items.text: AMP Validator
Google Search Console

content.text: For more details, refer to the AMP Official Documentation .

title: Giving Access In Google Analytics

content.text: To give access to your Google Analytics account, you can follow these steps:
Step 1

items.text: Sign in to your Google Analytics account
Click on the Admin tab in the navigation menu

content.text: Step 2

items.text: Under the "Account" section, select the account that you want to give access to
Click “Account User Management.”

content.text: Step 3

items.text: Click the blue + to add a new user to your account
Enter the email address
Select "Notify new users by email" for the person to receive the invite
Set roles as shown below for the member

content.text: Here are the options

items.text: Administrator: Grants complete control over the Google Analytics dashboard, from the account, property, and view levels
Editor: Allows the user to edit anything within the account, except for user settings
Analyst: Works like a Viewer role ( below ), but with the ability to make and edit shared items like dashboards
Viewer: Can view all settings and data on an account, but can’t edit or share anything
None: This is a role, but without any abilities, perhaps used for someone you intend to add in the future

title: How to configure Banner and Announcement for website

sections.heading: We are thrilled to announce that the updated version of Banner and Announcement is now live on the dashboard. To configure it, navigate to the Configuration section and select Banner and Announcement, as shown below.

content.text: This feature allows publishers to update, arrange, and remove banners and announcements on website pages directly.
Note: Only Owners and Admins can access this configuration section.

sections.heading: How to Set Up Banner and Announcement

content.text: To set up Banner and Announcement, go to the sidebar and click on the Configuration section.
To get started with Banner and Announcement Configuration, go to the Banner and Announcement section.
Here we have certain fields which are Position, Banner Slot Name, Status, Slot type, Updated on, Updated by and Actions.

items.text: Position : It shows the slot position, which can be used to visualise using a slot image.

content.text: Note : Position has a standard naming convention for slots, which defines the position. The slot name consists of two words: the first word indicates the device, and the second word indicates the page. "HP" stands for Home Page, "CP" stands for Category Page, and "AP" stands for Article Page. The last word indicates the position.
For example, "Mobile_ap_top" indicates that this slot is for the mobile version
on the article page in the top position. as shown in slot icon.

items.text: Banner Slot Name : It describes slot names, as we can have multiple slots in a single slot position so it helps to differentiate.
Status : It shows if the slot is activated or inactivated, we can change the status of the slot using the toggle button.
Updated on : It shows the last updated date.
Updated by : It shows details of the User who made updates recently.
Actions : Using this section you can edit the slot.
Slot type : it shows the slot type applied, we currently have 3 different types of slots which are Image type, Rich Text Snippet and Custom Code Snippet.

content.text: Slot Types

Image Type : You can upload an image of different sizes with a URL. To upload an image, follow the media upload feature. Enter the target URL and select the size according to the device. The slot status is active by default. If you don’t want it to be live immediately, you can change the status to inactive. Overrides on category and article pages are also possible.

Rich Text Snippet : Use this for notifications. Enter the notification content in the content field. The slot status is active by default. If you don’t want it to be live immediately, you can change the status to inactive. Overrides on category and article pages are also possible.

Custom Code Snippet : Paste any code (e.g., GAM tag or other scripts) into the head or body. Enter the code in the content field. The slot status is active by default. If you don’t want it to be live immediately, you can change the status to inactive. Overrides on category and article pages are also possible.
To set an override, click on "Add Override."

Overrides only affect the selected slot type. They can be based on categories, tags, or authors, providing flexibility in content management. You can select at least one of these criteria to customize the override settings. For example, if you choose categories, the override will apply specifically to the content within those categories. Similarly, if you select tags or authors, the override will be tailored to the specified tags or authors. This allows for precise control over the content displayed in the selected slot.
Category Override : If you select a category, the override will apply to that category only. Other articles will remain the same.
Tags & Author Override : If you select tags, the override will apply to any article associated with those tags or authors.
Once you upload an image or fill in the required fields, you can save it, and it will be reflected on the website.
Note : In starting, this section is blank. You need to raise a ticket to activate the required slots before configuring the banner and announcement section.

title: Zapier Integration

sections.heading: Building Webhooks for Publive: Empowering Publisher Integration

content.text: Publive introduces powerful webhooks designed to streamline interactions and enhance operational efficiency for publishers. These webhooks enable seamless integration with external systems, allowing publishers to stay informed and take immediate action on critical events within Publive.

Webhooks in Publive function by triggering HTTP POST requests to user-defined URLs, providing real-time notifications whenever specified events occur. These events range from content updates to member activities, ensuring that publishers can promptly respond to changes and updates in their ecosystem.
Setting up a webhook in Publive is straightforward and can be managed directly through the Publive Configuration user interface. Key components include:

items.text: Target URL : This is the endpoint where Publive will send POST requests. It must be a publicly accessible URL.
Events : Publishers can subscribe to various events to trigger webhook notifications. Examples include content updates, member activities, and more.

content.text: Upon triggering an event, Publive sends a JSON payload to the configured endpoint, containing detailed information about the event. This data can facilitate actions ranging from simple notifications to complex system integrations.
Publive supports a comprehensive range of events that publishers can subscribe to via webhooks:

items.text: Content Management : Events related to posts, tags, categories, and site changes.
Member Activities : Events such as member additions, edits, and deletions.
Reader Engagement : Includes activities like newsletter subscriptions, downloads, and interactions with paywall content.
User-Generated Content (UGC) : Notifications for submissions, approvals, and publication of user-generated content.
Navigation Updates : Updates to website navigation elements like navbar and footer.
Notifications : Events related to push notifications and newsletter campaigns.

content.text: Publive web hooks seamlessly integrate with Zapier, empowering publishers to automate workflows and connect Publive events with over thousands of other apps. For instance, when a new article is published ( post.published ), Zapier can automatically trigger actions based on predefined rules, such as sending notifications or updating external databases.
When a post is published ( post.published ), the webhook payload includes essential metadata about the article, such as:
post.published:

{ "title": "testing batching for newsletter locks", "legacy_url": "/ignore/testing-batching-for-newsletter-locks-2739", "banner_url": "https://img-cdn.thepublive.com/demo/media/media_files/CphhvD4a3E1B736yCjXy.png", "type": "Article", "status": "Published", "absolute_url": "https://demo.lvh.me:8081/ignore/testing-batching-for-newsletter-locks-2739" }
This data enables integrations to immediately act upon published content, enhancing publisher productivity and engagement.

Publive’s robust webhook system empowers publishers with real-time insights and operational efficiency, ensuring seamless integration with external systems and enabling swift action on critical events. By leveraging Publive webhooks, publishers can automate workflows, enhance user engagement, and stay ahead in a dynamic digital publishing landscape.

Step 2 : Navigate to the Team or Add Team Members Step 3 : Click "Add Team member" to open the Create Member page Step 4: Fill in the required details: Email (Mandatory) Name (Mandatory) Slug (Mandatory – enter the team member's unique identifier) Role (Mandatory – select the Contributor role) Meta Title & Meta Description (Optional but recommended) Author Display Order (Default: 1000) Description (optional) Hide Author on Website (if do not want to show the name on the website turn off the button) Add Social Profiles (optional) Step 5 :Click "Save" to add the team member. Step 6: Deleting a Member Click the delete icon under the actions column. Click "Delete" to confirm the deletion but Deletion is not possible when this member has posts that exist. title: Advanced SEO Modules : SEO Meta Configurations

content.text: The SEO meta patterns section provides guidance on following best practices for creating meta tags, including title tags and meta descriptions. You can use the codes we have provided or create your own as per your requirements. These patterns are designed to enhance a website’s search engine optimization by improving search engine rankings, increasing click-through rates (CTR), and boosting overall user engagement. Where to Find the Feature The Advanced SEO Module feature is seamlessly integrated into the Configuration Section of the Dashboard . Feature Availability The Advanced SEO Module feature is available to Owner and Admins users across all accounts. This feature is available for plans Advanced and higher Getting Started with the Feature Step 1: Login in to Dashboard Step 2: Go to Configurations, inside Advanced SEO Modules choose Misc.
Step 3: Inside Misc. , go to SEO Meta
Step 4: Apply fallback meta patterns to all category pages, tag pages and author pages for uniform metadata across categories and Save it.
*Note : The homepage is already optimized, and for all posts, publishers can set up meta values at the post level.
meta patterns for all category pages
For "All Category" Title Patterns you may set the pattern as following

{{category.name}} | {{publisher.name}} For "All Category" Description Patterns you may set the pattern as following Explore the latest in {{category.name}} on {{publisher.name}}. Stay informed with top stories and in-depth coverage. meta patterns for all tag pages For "All Tags" Title Patterns you may set the pattern as following {{tag.name}} | {{publisher.name}} For "All Tags" Description Patterns you may set the pattern as following Explore the latest in {{tag.name}} on {{publisher.name}}. Stay informed with top stories and in-depth coverage. meta patterns for all author pages For "All Author" Title Patterns you may set the pattern as following {{author.name}} | {{publisher.name}} For "All Author" Description Patterns you may set the pattern as following Explore the latest in {{author.name}} on {{publisher.name}}. Stay informed with top stories and in-depth coverage. Benefits for Digital Publishers Improved Search Engine Visibility: Well-optimized meta tags (title, and description) increase the chances of ranking higher in search results, driving more organic traffic. Increased Click-Through Rates (CTR): Compelling meta titles and descriptions encourage users to click on your links by making your content stand out among search results. Efficient Content Indexing by Search Engines: Meta tags (e.g., canonical tags, robots meta tags) help search engines understand the structure of your website and avoid issues like duplicate content.
